meaning: n.故意怠慢,斥责v.故意怠慢,责骂,压熄(香烟等),冷落慢待
examples: Ryan took it as a snub.
Her refusal to attend the dinner is being seen as a deliberate snub to the President.
Scientists made an unexpected discovery in the waters south of New Guinea in 2005: a new species of dolphin called the snub-fin.
I'm taking a morose tone at this point with Garrison Keillor giving me the snub.
But the lack of an Oscar nomination was widely seen as a major snub.
